title: aloysia virgata var. laxa
intro: Aloysia virgata var. laxa, also known as Lippia or Mexican oregano, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including the ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and support the immune system. Aloysia virgata var. laxa can be prepared for medicinal use through teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin irritations.
